name: Culingionis Con Cirubba
recipeIngredient: 4 Medium red onions, diced into 1/4 inch pieces
2 Medium potatoes, peeled and diced into 1/4 inch cubes
recipeInstructions:
1. Heat olive oil in a 12 to 14-inch saute pan until it begins to smoke.
2. Add onions and potatoes and cook until they are softened and lightly browned, about 8 to 10 minutes.
3. Add vinegar and sugar and bring the mixture to a boil.
4. Continue cooking until the mixture is almost dry, about 15 minutes.
5. Allow the mixture to cool and stir in mint leaves.
6. Roll out pasta on a floured surface to the thinnest setting on the pasta roller.
7. Cut the dough into 3-inch disks.
8. Place 1 tablespoon of onion mixture in the center of half the disks.
9. Use the other half to cover and pinch the sides together, repeating until all filling is used up.
10. Bring 6 quarts water to a boil and add 2 tablespoons salt.
11. Add culingionis and cook until tender, about 3 to 4 minutes.
12. Melt butter in a 12 to 14-inch saute pan.
13. Drain the pasta carefully and place it in the pan with butter.
14. Add mint leaves and half the cheese and toss.
15. Serve immediately.
Yield: 4 servings
